authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Noyo River Reach A (mouth to Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge)
reaches.fromDesc: Mouth of the Noyo River at Noyo Harbor, Fort Bragg
reaches.toDesc: Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge, about one mile east of Highway 1

reaches.name: Noyo River Reach B (GP bridge to South Fork Noyo confluence)
reaches.fromDesc: Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge, about one mile east of Highway 1
reaches.toDesc: Confluence with the South Fork Noyo River

reaches.name: Noyo River Reach C (South Fork confluence to Camp Noyo)
reaches.fromDesc: Confluence with the South Fork Noyo River
reaches.toDesc: Sonoma/Mendocino Boy Scout Council Camp (Camp Noyo)

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(70) — Noyo River (Mendocino Co.)
sources.quotedText: (70) Noyo River (Mendocino Co.). Also see Section 8.00(b) Low-Flow Restrictions. (A) Noyo River main stem from the mouth to the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge one mile east of Highway 1. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: 14 CCR §8.00(b) — Mendocino, Sonoma, and Marin County Coastal Streams: Special Low Flow Conditions Closures
sources.quotedText: (b) Mendocino, Sonoma, and Marin County coastal streams: Stream Closures: Special Low Flow Conditions.

sources.title: Fort Bragg / Noyo River Fishing Guide — California Fish Plants
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

groups.humanSummary: Noyo River main stem from the mouth to the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge one mile east of Highway 1: open fourth Saturday in May through March 31, artificial lures with barbless hooks only through October 31 then barbless hooks only through March 31, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession).
groups.verbatimText: (A) Noyo River main stem from the mouth to the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge one mile east of Highway 1. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: Noyo River main stem from the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge to the confluence with the South Fork Noyo River: open fourth Saturday in May through October 1, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession).
groups.verbatimText: (B) Noyo River main stem from the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge one mile east of Highway 1 to the confluence with the South Fork Noyo River. Fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 1. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: Noyo River main stem from the confluence with the South Fork Noyo River to the Sonoma/Mendocino Boy Scout Council Camp: open fourth Saturday in May through March 31, artificial lures with barbless hooks only through October 31 then barbless hooks only through March 31, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession).
groups.verbatimText: (C) Noyo River main stem from the confluence with the South Fork Noyo River to the Sonoma/Mendicino Boy Scout Council Camp. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.description: This water is also subject to 14 CCR §8.00(b), the Mendocino/Sonoma/Marin County coastal streams special low-flow closure provision, under which fishing may be closed when streamflow at a designated gauge falls below a prescribed threshold.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to Section 8.00(b) low-flow stream closures.
Open fourth Saturday in May through March 31, from the mouth to the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge.
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Applies from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31, from the mouth to the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31.

parameters.note: Applies November 1 through March 31; bait is allowed on barbless hooks.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used from November 1 through March 31, from the mouth to the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge.
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, from the mouth to the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Open fourth Saturday in May through October 1, from the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge to the confluence with the South Fork Noyo River.
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 1.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, for the full open season on this reach.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, from the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge to the confluence with the South Fork Noyo River.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, from the Georgia-Pacific logging road bridge to the confluence with the South Fork Noyo River.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Open fourth Saturday in May through March 31, from the confluence with the South Fork Noyo River to the Sonoma/Mendocino Boy Scout Council Camp.
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Applies from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31, from the South Fork Noyo confluence to Camp Noyo.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31.

parameters.note: Applies November 1 through March 31; bait is allowed on barbless hooks.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used from November 1 through March 31, from the South Fork Noyo confluence to Camp Noyo.
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, from the South Fork Noyo confluence to Camp Noyo.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
